title: centaurea dufourii subsp. resupinata
intro: Centaurea dufourii subsp. resupinata, also known as the Dufour's knapweed or small-flowered knapweed, is a subspecies of perennial plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Centaurea dufourii subsp. resupinata include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, skin irritations, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to act as a diuretic to relieve urinary problems. Traditionally, the plant is prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and alleviate associated health issues.
